claim: d=2 Newton-polygon proof statement (xi_0 = 2/sqrt(beta_2), rho = -3/2 - beta_1/beta_2): cited verbatim from Channel Theory v1.3 Proposition 3.3.A and Worked Example, recorded earlier in PCF-1 v1.3 Theorem 5 / Section 5; canonical source for d=2 is the CT v1.3 form. PROVEN tier.

d=4 verification fact: 8 quartic representatives (S_4 generic, D_4 Eisenstein anchor, V_4 biquadratic anchor, beta_4=7 sample) hit xi_0(b) = 4 / beta_4^(1/4) at dps=80 with spread 0; the inferred c(4)=4 is a measurement, not a derivation. VERIFIED tier (forbidden verbs avoided in body text). Source: PCF2-SESSION-Q1 claim Q1-B in sessions/2026-05-01/PCF2-SESSION-Q1/claims.jsonl line 4, script session_q1_newton.py.

Falsification of the v1.1 candidate c(d) = 2*sqrt((d-1)!) at d=4: the v1.1 form predicts c(4) = 2*sqrt(6) ~ 4.899, against the PCF2-SESSION-Q1 measurement c(4)=4 with spread 0 at dps=80, a ~22% disagreement four orders of magnitude above the per-representative resolution. Cited from CT v1.3 Remark 3.3.E.

Conjecture 3.3.A* statement: xi_0(b) = d / beta_d^(1/d) for every PCF (1, b) in scope with deg b = d >= 2, beta_d > 0. Status in this note explicitly labelled CONJECTURED for general d (proven at d=2, verified at d=4, deferred at d=3). Cited verbatim from CT v1.3 Conjecture 3.3.A*.

d=3 verification status: deferred to op:xi0-d3-direct (one Newton-polygon test per Galois bin on a cubic representative at dps=80, predicting xi_0 = 3 / beta_3^(1/3); tractability 1-2 hours per bin, no halt expected). DEFERRED tier. Cited from CT v1.3 section 9 op:xi0-d3-direct.
